prompt: Three-quarter close detail of a small stack of stapled multi-page contract documents resting on a kitchen table, a brass-barrel fountain pen lying diagonally across the top page. The visible pages show the standard layout of a legal contract — paragraph blocks, indented numbered clauses, a signature line with an X-marker hint above it — but the printed text is intentionally rendered as pure visual texture, abstract horizontal ink blocks, no legible letters or words. A wax seal impression in red on one corner as a non-saturated touch of color. Shot with a 50mm normal lens at a shallow three-quarter angle, soft warm dusk window light from camera-left, slight golden cast, gentle shadow falloff. Slightly desaturated palette, the brand red on a small Stanley utility knife in the foreground is the fully saturated color. Working-contractor catalog feel, NOT HGTV bright. No readable text.
